programs.name: CampTO Plus: Cooking
programs.description: In this camp, you will learn the art of cooking. On the menu are basic skills like measuring, cutting, and mixing as well as kitchen safety, nutrition and healthy eating.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your cooking skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.Efforts will be made to accommodate dietary restrictions but we cannot make any guarantees.

programs.description: Get ready to explore the great outdoors. You will learn about and connect with the environment through hiking, crafts and nature games.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on the  environment, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. programs.name: CampTO Plus: Musical Theatre
programs.description: Get ready to take the stage! Through a mixture of dance, drama and music instruction, campers will learn the art of performance.  You will sing, learn choreography and practice scenes to prepare for the end-of-session show.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your musical theatre skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Sports
programs.description: Calling all sports fans! In this camp you will have the opportunity to learn the fundamentals of play in a variety of sports. Through drills, practice and games, enhance your skills and have some fun. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your sport skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: Cardio Dance
programs.description: Cardio dance is a dynamic and exciting aerobic workout that anyone can enjoy. With a focus on various culturally inspired music and dance movements and a goal of lifting your heart rate you will dance your way to new fitness heights.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your skills in basketball, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.
programs.location: Joseph J. Piccininni Cmty Rec Centre

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Dance Basics
programs.description: Explore and enjoy a variety of dance styles including ballet, jazz, hip hop and contemporary. As you practice basic steps and proper technique, you will build on flexibility, strength and endurance.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your dance skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.
programs.location: Joseph J. Piccininni Cmty Rec Centre

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Environmental Adventure
programs.description: Get ready to explore the great outdoors. You will learn about and connect with the environment through hiking, crafts and nature games.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on the  environment, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Soccer
programs.description: Calling all soccer fans! Through practice and play, you will build on skills like kicking, passing and heading while learning the rules of the game an practicing respectful play.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your soccer skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics, or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.name: Extended Hours: AM
programs.description: This option provides supervised care before the start of the camp day for children registered in a City of Toronto Parks, Forestry and Recreation camp.Extended hours activities have a separate barcode for registration. Please note that campers must be registered at the same location for extended hours as the activity they have registered in. Refer to the camp program charts for times, fees and locations.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Learn To Skate with Caregiver
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun games and activities, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping. A caregiver who is at least 14 years of age and a competent skater must actively participate in this activity.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage (for the child) and a CSA-approved hockey helmet (for the caregiver). All skaters require hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Meditation
programs.description: Meditation is a practice of mental well-being. Combining basic meditation techniques with restorative breathing you will learn to relax the mind and body.

programs.name: National Lifeguard - Pool
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. National Lifeguard education is designed to develop a sound understanding of lifeguarding principles, good judgment and a mature and responsible attitude toward the role of the lifeguard. The course develops the basic lifeguarding skills, principles and decision-making processes that will assist the lifeguard to evaluate and adapt to different aquatic facilities and emergencies.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old, and Bronze Cross, and Standard First Aid with CPR-C certification (current or expired) from an approved provider.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: National Lifeguard - Pool Recert
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. This recertification course offers candidates with certification that will be expiring the opportunity to maintain current certification at the National Lifeguard level in a pool setting. Your lifeguarding skills will be tested based on physical standards, judgment and skill.Prerequisite: National Lifeguard - Pool (current or expired).Equipment: Bring a whistle.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Sports
programs.description: Calling all sports fans! In this camp you will have the opportunity to learn the fundamentals of play in a variety of sports. Through drills, practice and games, enhance your skills and have some fun. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your sport skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or onsite special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: Cardio Dance
programs.description: Cardio dance is a dynamic and exciting aerobic workout that anyone can enjoy. With a focus on various culturally inspired music and dance movements and a goal of lifting your heart rate you will dance your way to new fitness heights.

programs.name: Cardio High/Low
programs.description: Do you love aerobic exercise? With the ability to choose between high and low-impact movements, in this workout, you decide what works best for you to improve your overall fitness.

programs.name: Club (Girls)
programs.description: Share in the fun and meet new people in your age group. Here you will enjoy socializing and talking about shared interests as you participate in activities you will choose together. This activity is facilitated by staff, there is no formal instruction. This activity is for all girls.

programs.name: Emergency First Aid (CPR B)
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. Candidates will learn first-aid principles and the emergency treatment of injuries. This course teaches victim assessment, CPR, choking and how to respond to external bleeding, heart attack, stroke, wounds and burns.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

Extended hours activities have a separate barcode for registration. Please note that campers must be registered at the same location for extended hours as the activity they have registered in. Refer to the camp program charts for times, fees and locations.
